title: Baked Feta Salmon Pasta
imagePrompt: A shallow baking dish holds short pasta coated in a creamy, blush-pink sauce — the collapsed cherry tomatoes have burst into jammy pockets of red and orange threaded through the pasta. Wilted spinach weaves through in dark green ribbons. Large, tender chunks of salmon rest on top, their flesh pale rose and just beginning to flake at the edges, glistening where the feta sauce clings to them.
subtitle: Salmon and feta bake together with cherry tomatoes, spinach, and pesto into a silky one-dish sauce.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Everything bakes together—the feta melts into a creamy sauce with the tomato juices.
ingredients.callout: Key tip: Reserve pasta water before draining—the starch emulsifies the feta sauce.

body.html: Salmon fillets bake alongside a block of feta, cherry tomatoes, garlic, and spinach. Everything melds in the oven, then gets stirred together with pesto and pasta water into a rich, creamy sauce. Toss with pasta, flake the salmon on top.

items: Assemble baking dish with feta, tomatoes, garlic, spinach
Build the sauce—mash feta, squeeze roasted garlic, stir
Toss pasta in sauce, top with flaked salmon

body.html: One pan: Everything bakes in a single dish. Minimal cleanup.

items: Season with onion powder, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Set aside while you assemble the baking dish.

slides.title: Assemble the Baking Dish
slides.checkboxLabel: Feta, tomatoes, garlic, spinach in dish

items: Place feta block in the center of a large baking dish.
Scatter cherry tomatoes and garlic cloves (unpeeled) around the feta.
Nestle the seasoned salmon fillets in the dish alongside the feta.

body.html: Leave garlic unpeeled: The cloves roast in their skins and turn sweet and soft. Squeeze them out after baking.

items: Salmon should be cooked through and flaking at the edges.
Feta should be soft and lightly golden.
While it bakes, cook pasta in well-salted water.
Reserve ½ cup pasta water before draining.

body.html: Don't skip the pasta water: The starch is what turns the baked feta into a pourable, creamy sauce.

items: Squeeze garlic cloves out of their skins—discard skins.
Mash the feta and stir it together with the tomatoes, roasted garlic, and spinach.
Add pasta water a splash at a time until the sauce is loose and creamy.

body.html: Texture check: Sauce should be creamy and pourable, not thick like a dip. Keep adding pasta water until it coats a spoon.

slides.title: Combine + Serve
slides.checkboxLabel: Toss pasta in sauce, top with salmon

items: Add drained pasta directly into the baking dish.
Toss to coat in the feta sauce.
Flake the salmon into large chunks and lay on top.

body.html: Option: Keep salmon whole on top for presentation, or fold it in for a more mixed dish.
